_meta.what: Cutting-edge / representative freshwater cyanoHAB research MODELS. Each attribute traces to a source dossier key (resolves in ../../Research/REFERENCES.md). 'n/r' = not reported.
_meta.emphasis: Features/datasets used and which are MOST important. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: feature 'importance' here = how often a feature is a model's top-ranked predictor, a correlational, model-internal finding — NOT a causal driver. Water temperature is frequently top-ranked but is often a seasonal-cycle proxy; some models rank phycocyanin or nutrients/geolocation first.
_meta.feature_figure_rule: The feature-frequency figure counts a model toward a feature only if features_reported=true AND the feature is in its 'features' list (from its dossier). Review/surrogate/sensor-benchmark entries and abstract-only entries with no stated feature list have features_reported=false and are excluded from the count (N stated on the figure).
_meta.model_universe: 15 inventory entries = 14 nowcasting/forecasting MODELS (is_model=true) + 1 literature review (M13/ACAD-068, is_model=false, shown as context). Of the 14 models, 10 report an environmental feature list (features_reported=true).
_meta.phycocyanin_note: phycocyanin_signal = the cyanobacteria-specific blue pigment's optical signal; used in satellite EO (abs ~620nm) but in ACAD-037 it is an IN-SITU fluorescence-probe measurement (a direct bloom-abundance proxy). Grouped with the bloom-signal class.

models.name: Bayesian spatiotemporal fusion (INLA), 2,192 US lakes
models.skill: Out-of-sample 2021: 90% acc, AUC 0.95, 88% sens, 91% spec, but 49% precision (overpredicts). Note: source has an unresolved internal sample-count discrepancy
models.note: The operational EPA 7-day forecast IS this model (also Slide-1 tool).

models.name: R-INLA spatial (SPDE) + AR1, 103 Florida lakes
models.skill: AUC 0.95 holdout -> 0.89 true 1-week forecast; spatial structure gave the largest fit gain; AR1 alpha=0.90 (strong persistence)
models.note: Shows persistence (prior state) is a large part of near-term skill; authors don't expect accuracy beyond ~2 weeks.

models.name: Explainable LSTM + SHAP, 102 sites
models.skill: mean NSE 0.48 (median 0.52), beat 8 ML baselines; but 30% of sites 'poor' (NSE<0.3) - highly site-dependent
models.note: Water temperature = top SHAP driver nationally (11.7%). In-situ + ERA5 reanalysis; no satellite.

models.name: Interpretable CatBoost + SHAP, Yilong Lake
models.note: Abstract-level detail; full feature list only partially reported.

models.name: Intelligible ML early-warning, Lake Müggelsee
models.skill: Tree/rule models generalized; LSTM failed to generalize on daily data. NOTE: under a published Comment/rebuttal [ACAD-110]
models.note: EXCEPTION to 'temperature is #1': phycocyanin ranks first here (temperature 2nd). Illustrates feature importance is model- and site-specific.

models.name: ANN vs SVM reservoir alert-level
models.algorithm: Artificial neural network / support vector machine
models.temporal: forecast (6-7 day = efficient sampling interval, not lead time)
models.note: Specific input variables not reported in accessible abstract (features n/r).

models.name: RF + boosting ensemble vs CNN/ANN
models.note: ~9 yr met/hydro/water-quality data; specific top features n/r.

models.name: NOAA Lake Erie 3-D transport (mechanistic)
models.skill: Transport formulations compared vs satellite bloom fields over multiple seasons
models.note: Satellite-initialized + physics. Companion toxin model [ACAD-051] forecasts 7-day microcystin TRANSPORT/fate (not early warning). Research side of NOAA Erie product.

models.name: Multi-source fusion severity classifier (Sentinel-2 + DEM + climate)
models.algorithm: Random forest + LightGBM + multi-input neural net (ensemble)
models.skill: 6th of field (RA-RMSE 0.81 vs 0.76 winner); 62.8% exact-class, 89.5% severe-case accuracy
models.note: TOP feature = longitude/geolocation, a regionally-learned, explicitly NON-transferable correlational proxy (NOT a driver). Temp/precip/wind are contributing climate inputs. Simpler segmentation-based entries beat this fused ensemble.

models.name: WASP eutrophication process model, Lake Winnipeg
models.note: Represents N & P cycling + 3 phytoplankton groups (incl. N-fixers). Process-based, not data-driven.

models.name: Frontier synthesis: 168-study cross-review (1997-2025)
models.skill: Reports deep learning (esp. LSTM) strongest median (R^2 0.89, >90% acc in many apps); hybrids highest accuracy; flags limited transferability across water bodies
models.note: Best single 'state of the frontier' anchor; do not treat any single in-study accuracy as a general benchmark.

models.name: Deep-learning surrogate of a process model (hybrid)
models.skill: NSE 0.644 -> 0.930 vs the process model it emulates
models.note: Emerging hybrid strategy: ML surrogate makes an expensive mechanistic model fast.

models.name: NASA PACE hyperspectral cyanoHAB validation
models.skill: R^2 0.84 vs operational CyAN benchmark; ~halved chl-a retrieval error (NRMSE 21.6% vs 38.7%)
models.note: Cutting-edge SENSOR (2026); detection/retrieval benchmark, not a forecast model. Excluded from feature-frequency count.
